summary: DHH celebrates the arrival of Veta Universal 1.0, software that enables Nokia 3650 owners to control their Mac over Bluetooth, a capability previously limited to SonyEricsson phone owners. He had long envied the ability to use a phone as an iTunes and Keynote remote. While the $8 shareware only works with the free but limited Romeo app rather than the superior Clicker, he praises it as the only available solution for Nokia 3650 users. He notes minor annoyances with the PayPal-only registration process.
key_points: Bluetooth phone-to-Mac control was an exciting early application of Bluetooth technology, useful for iTunes and Keynote remoting
Veta Universal 1.0 bridges the gap by translating SonyEricsson API calls into Series 60 (Nokia) format
The free Romeo app lacks features compared to the $9.99 Clicker, but is the only option that works with Veta
PayPal-only registration is noted as an inconvenient friction point for purchasing shareware
money_quotes: Controlling your Mac from your phone over Bluetooth has got to be one of the coolest applications of the latter.
On top of being cool, it's also Really Useful. Especially as a iTunes and Keynote remote.
But let's not talk down a good thing.
key_insight: An early tech enthusiasm post showing DHH's excitement about Bluetooth phone-to-Mac control, a novel capability in 2003 that foreshadowed the smartphone-as-remote paradigm.
